msgs.text:
I treated the prior investigations as proposals rather than proof. The prompt fixes the tame-format submission: sigma,tau are distinguished, the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 is implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and profinite exponents are reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs surviving candidate is still the dynamic tame-inertia word; the earlier scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is irrelevant here because that exponent is applied only to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.The two algebraic conventions from the recent turns should remain frozen as equations.For the sign-cover norm coefficient, with t the deck involution of V=kerőļ, and with ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block, the Fox coefficient forNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄisDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j,and it satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít.

Thus the usable formula isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the direction that makes the unit/radical argument valid: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0 up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. The elimination polynomial Ps (T) must be defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.

Writing f=2a m with m odd, s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). Then h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, since (1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1),and gcd(f,2s+1)=1. This keeps the prior abelianization and sign-kernel conclusions coherent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.My new contribution is a gauge correction to Turn 57‚Äôs proposed arithmetic comparison. Turn 57 correctly says that H1(P,L2H) is a difference space, not a space of raw coordinates for a single marked Frobenius lift. But there is an additional gauge that was not fully isolated: the choice of normal-basis generator for the regular summand F2 [őĒ]. This gauge acts nontrivially on the involution-indexed Zj -directions.LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,with e odd and e‚ą£2f‚ąí1. Put f=2a m, m odd, and

H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü©,P=őĒ/H‚ČÉC2a.For f even, let n=f/2. The involutions in őĒ areőīj=ŌĄňČjŌÉňČn,j(1+2n)‚Č°0(mode).Let

K=ker(1+2n:Ce‚ÜíCe),‚ą£K‚ą£=gcd(e,2n+1).

The corresponding first nonabelian directions areZj=h‚ąąH‚ąĎ[Xh,Xhőīj],j‚ąąK.

Now change the regular normal-basis generator by a unitu=a‚ąąK‚ąĎuaŌĄňČa‚ąąF2[K]√ó,so, at the Frattini level,Xg‚Ü¶Xg‚Ä≤=a‚ąąK‚ąĎuaXgŌĄňČa.

Then the involution directions transform byZj‚Ü¶Zj‚Ä≤=a‚ąąK‚ąĎuaZj‚ąí2a.The proof is a direct calculation. Since őījŌĄňČb=ŌĄňČj+2nbŌÉňČn, and 2n=‚ąí1 on K, a term[XhŌĄňČa,XhőījŌĄňČb]has new involution index j‚ąía‚ąíb. In characteristic 2, the off-diagonal pairs (a,b) and (b,a) cancel in the alternating bracket sum, leaving only a=b, hence j‚ąí2a.

Thus the space spanned by the Zj ‚Äôs is not a coordinate space with canonically labeled basis vectors. It is the group algebra F2 [K], acted on by F2[K]√óthrough Frobenius-twisted multiplication. Since K has odd order, F2 [K] is semisimple; its unit orbits are classified by which irreducible factors of X ‚ą£K‚ą£‚ąí1 have zero component, not by named coefficients Zj.

In the key test case (e,f)=(5,4), one has K=C5andF2[C5]‚ČÉF2‚äēF16.So the five involution coordinates split into the invariant lineN=Z0+Z1+Z2+Z3+Z4 and a four-dimensional augmentation component. The normal-basis unit group F16√óacts transitively on the nonzero augmentation vectors. Therefore an arithmetic computation in (5,4) should not compare five named Zj -coordinates unless the regular normal basis has also been fixed, not merely the Frobenius lift and the b-lift.This does not weaken Turn 57‚Äôs ‚Äúarithmetic-minus-candidate should vanish‚ÄĚ target: zero is still a gauge-invariant target. But it prevents a false negative. A nonzero-looking vector in the Zj -coordinates may be an artifact of comparing the arithmetic and candidate actions in different regular normal bases. The next arithmetic test must either fix the regular normal basis explicitly or compare only the gauge-invariant orbit in F2[K].

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I added a new normal-basis gauge calculation: for the involution-indexed first IA directions,Zj‚Ü¶a‚ąąK‚ąĎuaZj‚ąí2a under the regular generator change Xg‚Ü¶‚ąĎa‚ąąKuaXgŌĄňČa, u‚ąąF2[K]√ó. In (e,f)=(5,4), the five Zj -coordinates reduce, up to this gauge, to an invariant-line component and a zero/nonzero augmentation component.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing step remains an arithmetic comparison or dyadic formation theorem showing that the actual local Galois action has the same filtered slice origins as the candidate over every finite tame subextension. The next comparison must control Frobenius-lift gauge, b-lift gauge, and now regular normal-basis gauge.

NEXT: For (e,f)=(5,4), fix L=Q2(ő∂5,ŌĖ), ŌĖ5=2, with ŌÉ(ŌĖ)=ŌĖ, and also fix a specific normal-basis generator for the regular Kummer summand. Compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate class in H1(P,L2H). Report it in gauge-invariant form: the two square directions plus the F2‚äēF16 decomposition of the five involution directions.[BREAKTHROUGH]
